mission.title: backstage: 🐛 Bug Report: `publish:github:pull-request` fails with `HttpError: You have exceeded a secondary rate limit.`
mission.description: 🐛 Bug Report: `publish:github:pull-request` fails with `HttpError: You have exceeded a secondary rate limit.`. This issue affects 37+ users.

steps.title: Identify the publish:github:pull-request rate limit error
steps.description:
Check your Backstage application logs for the secondary rate limit error:
```bash
# If running Backstage locally via npm
npm run start 2>&1 | grep -i 'rate limit\|HttpError'

# If running Backstage via Docker
docker logs backstage --tail=100 | grep -i 'rate limit\|HttpError'
```
Look for `HttpError: You have exceeded a secondary rate limit` in scaffolder task logs. This occurs when the `publish:github:pull-request` action sends too many files in a single commit via the GitHub API.

steps.title: Review template configuration for large file sets
steps.description:
The root cause is that the scaffolder sends every file in the workspace as part of the pull request, even unchanged files. This triggers GitHub's secondary rate limit when repositories are large.

Review your scaffolder template to identify which `fetch:template` or `fetch:plain` steps pull in large directory trees. Check `app-config.yaml` for scaffolder configuration:
```bash
cat app-config.yaml | grep -A 20 scaffolder
```

steps.title: Apply the skeleton/flesh pattern workaround
steps.description:
Use the "skeleton/flesh" pattern to only send files that have changed. This involves fetching the existing repo content, then overlaying your template changes on top:

```yaml
steps:
  - id: fetch-repo
    name: Pull latest repo content
    action: fetch:plain
    input:
      url: https://github.com/${{ (parameters.repoUrl | parseRepoUrl).owner }}/${{ (parameters.repoUrl | parseRepoUrl).repo }}.git
      targetPath: flesh/repo

  - id: fetch-skeleton
    name: Fetch template skeleton (changed files only)
    action: fetch:template
    input:
      url: ./skeleton
      targetPath: flesh/repo
      values:
        name: ${{ parameters.name }}

  - id: publish-pr
    name: Create pull request with only changed files
    action: publish:github:pull-request
    input:
      repoUrl: ${{ parameters.repoUrl }}
      sourcePath: flesh/repo
      title: ${{ parameters.prTitle }}
```

This ensures only modified files are included in the PR, avoiding the rate limit. See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm the rate limit error is resolved
steps.description:
Re-run the scaffolder template and verify the pull request is created successfully:
1. Open the Backstage UI and navigate to the Software Templates page
2. Run the template that previously failed
3. Check the scaffolder task logs for successful PR creation
4. Verify the PR appears in your GitHub repository with only the expected changed files

resolution.summary: Our current workaround is to only send the files that have changed.

prerequisites.description: A running Backstage instance (local via npm, or Docker) with a scaffolder template using the publish:github:pull-request action.
